title: Lightly Crisp Australian Fish Sashimi
description: A refreshing Australian dish featuring sashimi-cut fish, ancho chillies and a hint of ayurvedic rajasic flavour.
instructions: Rinse the barramundi under cold water and pat dry with paper towels.
Place the barramundi on a plate or tray and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Grind the ancho chillies into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the powdered ancho chillies and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lime juice and honey (if using) to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Spoon the ancho chile glaze over the refrigerated barramundi and sprinkle with salt.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the barramundi to the skillet and sear for 1-2 minutes on each side, until lightly crisp.
Remove the barramundi from the skillet and let it cool to room temperature.
Cut the barramundi into sashimi-sized pieces and serve immediately, garnished with chopped cilantro.
